title: agents-version-control: GitHub for Agents
description: A guided walkthrough of the agents-version-control MCP server — a Cloudflare Workers demo that lets authenticated agents create repos, run preview builds, and manage pull requests.

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: This project is an MCP server running on Cloudflare Workers. It gives AI agents tools to manage Git repositories, seed Worker starter files, run preview builds, and merge pull requests. Built on Workers, Durable Objects, D1, and KV.

steps.title: Worker Entry & Configuration
steps.description: The Worker boots from a single entry point, with environment-driven configuration and the OAuth provider wired up alongside it. The Wrangler config declares the Durable Object, D1, and KV bindings the rest of the app depends on.

steps.title: MCP Agent Surface
steps.description: This is the agent-facing API. One module defines the MCP agent built on the Cloudflare Agents SDK, and another implements the tool methods agents call to manipulate repos.

steps.title: Per-Repo Durable Object
steps.description: A Durable Object owns per-repository state. Each repo gets a single coordinator instance, which keeps git operations, build runs, and pull request state safely serialized per repo.

steps.title: Services Layer
steps.description: The services layer holds the operational glue: a git wrapper running over an in-memory filesystem, a seeder that bootstraps new repos with starter files, sandboxed runners that execute preview and deploy builds, and an artifacts module that manages build outputs.

steps.title: D1 Database Layer
steps.description: Control-plane persistence lives here. One module defines the D1 tables, another wraps typed access patterns, and schema changes flow through the migrations directory.

steps.title: Tests & Tooling
steps.description: Tests run inside the Workers runtime via the Cloudflare vitest pool, so Durable Objects, D1, and KV bindings behave like production.

steps.title: Next Steps
steps.description: Run locally with the dev script and deploy with the deploy script. After changing bindings, regenerate Worker types. See the README for demo caveats — this is a proof-of-concept, not production-hardened.
